blocks.content: He was already at the fountain when she arrived.

Not waiting, exactly. Standing — hands in his pockets, eyes somewhere in the middle distance, the way he had of looking at things without quite landing on them, like he was always searching for the frame. He was facing the fountain. The morning light caught the planes of his face and he hadn't seen her yet, and she took one extra second before she said anything.
Just him. At the fountain. The very specific sight of someone who showed up.
She already knew he'd been here last night. She didn't know it yet, but she knew it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You're early.
Ethan turned. His expression did the thing she was starting to recognize — the slight settling, the half-second where his whole face just softened. Like something had confirmed itself.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I was in the neighborhood.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You live four blocks away.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Neighborhood.
She laughed and came to stand beside him. For a moment they both looked at the fountain — still sputtering, still uneven, still trying.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I thought about coming here last night.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I came here last night.
She looked at him. He looked at the fountain. Neither of them made anything of it.
They didn't have to.

The morning was that particular early hour where the city is still deciding what kind of day it wants to have. Cool, the kind that asked for a jacket. Light that hadn't made up its mind yet. A few people on the path with coffee cups, moving briskly, unbothered by the fountain or the two of them or anything.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Did you sleep?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Eventually.
She nodded. That was the right answer. An immediate yes would have been a lie and he didn't lie easily — she'd noticed that about him already. He delivered honest things in small, unhurried packages.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: So. Where are we going?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You're asking me?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You're the local artist. I want the tour.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm not a tour guide.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want the Ethan Carter version of this city.
She turned to face him fully.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: The version nobody else gets.
He was quiet for a moment — actually thinking, not performing a pause. She watched him consider it the way he considered everything: from the inside out.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: There's a gallery. Small. Most people walk past it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Obviously.
She smiled.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: That's the one.
